THIS BRIEF TORNADO BEGIN AT 6:29 PM CST AND ENDED AROUND 6:30 PM CST. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 0.20 MILE, HAD A WIDTH OF 25 YARDS, A MAXIMUM WIND SPEED OF 75 MPH, AND WAS RATED EF0. THERE WERE NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES. NO DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED. THE SIZE, STRENGTH, AND DURATION OF THIS BRIEF TORNADO WERE ESTIMATED BASED ON A COMBINATION OF VIDEO AND RADAR DATA.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN AT 6:41 PM CST ABOUT 8 MILES NORTHEAST OF TWITTY, TEXAS. IT HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 2.19 MILES, A PATH WIDTH OF 50 YARDS, AND AN ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED OF 110 MPH. THE TORNADO LIFTED AT AROUND 6:46 PM CST APPROXIMATELY 9 MILES NORTHEAST OF TWITTY, TEXAS. BASED ON DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING, THIS TORNADO WAS RATED EF1.

WIND DRIVEN HAIL CAUSED EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO HOUSE AND TREES. ALL WINDOWS ON NORTH SIDE OF HOUSE WERE BLOWN OUT. TREES WERE STRIPPED (EVEN BARK ON THE TREES WAS STRIPPED IN PLACES). HAIL ACCUMULATIONS OF 6-8 INCHES CAUSED DENSE HAIL FOG. WINTER WHEAT CROPS IN THE AREA WERE DESTROYED.

TORRENTIAL RAINFALL RATES OF 3 TO 5 INCHES PER HOUR RESULTED IN MAJOR STREET FLOODING THROUGHOUT TOWN. VEHICLES WERE STRANDED WITH SEVERAL WATER RESCUES. SOME STREETS HAD FAST FLOWING WATER OVER A FOOT DEEP COVERING THE STREET.

A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST TO THE EAST OF FM2373. IT CAUSED SOME BROKEN TREE LIMBS AND TOSSED A DUMPSTER SEVERAL YARDS BACK TO THE SOUTH. IT RESULTED IN DAMAGE TO AN ATTACHED GARAGE, COLLAPSING THE STRUCTURE. IT ALSO BLEW SOME HORSE TRAILERS OVER IN A NEARBY FIELD. THE TORNADO LIFTED IN AN EMPTY FIELD. MAX WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90MPH. TOTAL PATH LENGTH ESTIMATED AT 0.10 MILES AND WIDTH AT 25 YARDS.

Corrects previous flash flood report from Amarillo. Numerous reports of water covered roads with fast flowing water across town, becoming impassable in several areas. Drainage ditches and playa lakes overflowing onto roads and encroaching on homes in the southwest side of Amarillo.

Numerous reports of water covered roads with fast flowing water across town, becoming impassable in several areas. Drainage ditches and playa lakes overflowing onto roads and encroaching on homes in the southwest side of Amarillo.

Numerous road closures across the city due to flash flooding, playa lakes overflowing onto roads, water rescues ongoing with dive teams deployed. Several reports relayed from Amarillo Police Department and Amarillo Fire Department.
Multiple road closures in Canyon due to fast flowing water over roads. Reports relayed from emergency management and Canyon Police Department.

This tornado started just to the north of the Jiffy Cleaners along South Osage Road, where some tree limbs broke off a few trees. The top half of a tree snapped and fell alongside the cleaners building. A rusty air conditioning unit lifted from the roof of the cleaners and was tossed into the median of South Osage Road. The sign of Cliffside Coffee shop was blown out on the south side of the sign. The tornado crossed the street and appeared to gain a little more strength as it caused the brick siding of the Toot N Totum gas station to collapse. The tornado continued across SE 27th Avenue, where it caused minor damage to the Stars Drive-In and some cars in the parking lot had their windows blown out from debris. The tornado appeared to have dissipated in the field behind the drive-in. This tornado was documented by multiple people and we are grateful for their video evidence. Times were estimated based on video documentation and may be adjusted with further analysis and information. Estimated width was 10 yards and wind speed was 85mph.

Gas station canopy partially destroyed. Time estimated from radar.
Three high water rescues at the US 87 and US 60 interchange in Canyon. Hospital Drive, Hunsley Hills Boulevard, and Country Club Drive closed.

Flash flooding occurred in Pampa. Social media picture/video indicated fast moving water flowing down a residential street. Emergency management report an estimated three feet of water in some spots on the southside of Pampa.
Landspout spotted to the north of Panhandle. Ground circulation occurred from 1:21pm-1:38pm. No damage found. Movement was from east to west.
Short-lived landspout occurred east of the Amarillo airport.

Water was occurring over the roadways of 45th, Western and Bell Streets. Some locations have one to two feet of water across them. Some water was getting into yards on 45th Street. I-27 had water flowing down the interstate. Some water was threatening business on Western.

The Virginia Circle area was evacuated due to flooding. An estimated four feet of water was in the parking lot of the Oakridge Apartments and some buildings in the area were flooded. An estimated 130 people and 20 pets were evacuated per AFD. Multiple high water rescues were required. Cars were swept into ditches and submerged in water. Multiple roads in the area were closed. Multiple places in the area between Georgia Street and Paramount Boulevard, and from the Duniven Circle area to Duncan Street have standing water. Loop 335 east and westbound service road at Western as well as I-40 service road at Western were closed due to water over the roadway.

This tornado developed in an open field and was documented by storm chasers and trained spotters. Max length was 0.91 miles, width was estimated at 35 yards. It did not result in damage and was given an EFU rating.

This tornado touched down in southern Cimarron County and remained in an open field, resulting in no damage. It was witnessed by multiple storm spotters and chasers and was therefore given an EFU rating. Estimated length was 1.87 miles and width was 30 yards.

This tornado remained in an open field in the Rita Blanca National Grasslands. It was documented by several spotters and storm chasers but no damage otherwise occurred. It was given an EFU rating with an estimated 1.35 mile path length and 35 yards width.
